sn48.41:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in the Eastern Monastery, the palace of Migāra’s mother.
sn48.41:1.3: Now on that occasion the Blessed One, on emerging from his seclusion in the evening, sat warming his back in the western sun.
sn48.41:2.1: Then Ven. Ānanda went to the Blessed One and, on arrival, having bowed down to the Blessed One, massaged the Blessed One’s limbs with his hand and said,
sn48.41:2.3: how the Blessed One’s complexion is no longer so clear & bright; his limbs are flabby & wrinkled; his back, bent forward; there’s a discernible change in his faculties—
sn48.41:2.4: the faculty of the eye, the faculty of the ear, the faculty of the nose, the faculty of the tongue, the faculty of the body.”
sn48.41:3.2: When young, one is subject to aging; when healthy, subject to illness; when alive, subject to death.
sn48.41:3.3: The complexion is no longer so clear & bright; the limbs are flabby & wrinkled; the back, bent forward; there’s a discernible change in the faculties—
sn48.41:3.4: the faculty of the eye, the faculty of the ear, the faculty of the nose, the faculty of the tongue, the faculty of the body.”
sn48.41:4.1: That is what the Blessed One said.
sn48.41:4.2: Having said that, the One Well-Gone, the Teacher, said further:
sn48.41:5.1: “I spit on you, wretched old age—
sn48.41:5.2: old age that makes for ugliness.
sn48.41:6.1: Even those who live to a hundred
sn48.41:6.2: are headed—all—to an end in death,
